A handful of recurring research franchises — annual State-of-AI surveys, AI-readiness indexes, workforce studies, prediction series — supply most of the statistics in this knowledge base. Each ships new editions on a predictable cadence; when a newer edition lands, every page citing the prior one is a refresh candidate. This registry is the public record of which edition of each franchise the bundle currently relies on, and when to check for a newer one. *How current the underlying sources are is itself part of the provenance.*

**What "latest edition" means here:** the newest edition we have *verified and cite* — not a claim about the newest edition that exists. When a franchise is past its *stale-after* date, that is a prompt to go check for a newer edition, not an assertion that one is published. Confirming a new edition is semi-manual (these publishers block automated fetchers): a recency-filtered search surfaces a candidate, the operator verifies it, then updates the registry and the affected citations. Live freshness status is available to the operator via `evidence.py sources`.

## Registry

Stale-after rule of thumb from the edition date: quarterly +9 months, annual +18 months, biennial +36 months, irregular treated as annual.

### BCG

| Franchise | Cadence | Latest edition | Edition | Verified | Stale after |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| AI value series (Where's the Value → Widening Value Gap) | annual | 2025 | 2025-09 | 2026-06-20 | 2027-03 |
